name: dropletevaporationsimulation

metadata.name: DropletEvaporationSimulation
metadata.summary: This project simulates the evaporation and dynamics of an n-Decane droplet in a heated airflow.

documentation:
# Droplet Evaporation Simulation

[![Code style: black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg)](https://github.com/psf/black)
[![Coverage Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/guillaume-bailly/DropletEvaporationSimulation/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://coveralls.io/github/guillaume-bailly/DropletEvaporationSimulation?branch=main)
[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/dropletevaporationsimulation/badge/?version=latest)](https://dropletevaporationsimulation.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
![Test Status](https://github.com/guillaume-bailly/DropletEvaporationSimulation/actions/workflows/tests.yml/badge.svg)


This project simulates the evaporation and dynamics of an n-Decane droplet in a heated airflow, using both the DÂ² Law and Infinite Liquid Conductivity models. It visualizes droplet diameter, temperature, velocity, and axial position over time. 

It can be used to compute the minimal axial length of a combustion chamber to ensure full envaporation of the droplet.

Droplet properties can be modified in src/constants.py to simulate the vaporization of desired fuel.

## Features

- **DÂ² Law Model** and **Infinite Liquid Conductivity Model** simulations
- Plots for droplet diameter, temperature, axial velocity and position
- Adjustable parameters and configurations for droplet properties and environmental conditions

## Installation

```bash
git clone https://github.com/guillaume-bailly/DropletEvaporationSimulation.git
cd DropletEvaporationSimulation
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

## Documentation

https://dropletevaporationsimulation.readthedocs.io/en/latest/

functions.name: src.utils.interpolate_ode

Interpolates time dependant functions over a new time array

```
param t

_description_

type t

array

default ODE solution

type y

array

param ta

_description_

type ta

array

param f

time dependant function 1

type f

array

param g

time dependant function 2

type g

array

return

Returns the time derivative of the function

rtype

```

modules.name: src.main
documentation:
Main script for the Droplet Evaporation Simulation project.

This script initializes the simulation parameters, computes the evaporation
models, and generates plots for droplet properties over time.

modules.name: src.constants
documentation: This module initialize the constants. For a different fuel, the values can be changed under #Droplet properties.
src/constants.py

variables.name: src.constants.DO
documentation: Initial diameter (m)

variables.name: src.constants.RHO_F
documentation: Density (kg/m^3)

variables.name: src.constants.CP_F
documentation: Specific heat (J/kg/K)

variables.name: src.constants.H_FG
documentation: Latent heat of evaporation (J/kg)

variables.name: src.constants.DV
documentation: Mass diffusivity of vapor in air (m^2/s)

variables.name: src.constants.MF
documentation: Molecular weight (kg/mol)

variables.name: src.constants.TF
documentation: Initial temperature (K)

variables.name: src.constants.LAMBDA
documentation: Thermal conductivity (W/m/K)

variables.name: src.constants.RHO_A
documentation: Density (kg/m^3)

variables.name: src.constants.MU_A
documentation: Dynamic viscosity (Ns/m^2)

variables.name: src.constants.MA
documentation: Molecular weight (kg/mol)

variables.name: src.constants.TA
documentation: Far-field temperature (K)

variables.name: src.constants.CP_A
documentation: Specific heat (J/kg/K)

methods.name: src.simulation.DropletEvaporationModel.vapor_pressure
documentation:
Calculate vapor pressure based on temperature and model type.

```

Temperature

type T

Float

param model_type

Model type given Temperature (low or high), defaults to "low"

type model_type

str, optional

return

Vapor pressure

rtype

Float
```

methods.name: src.simulation.DropletEvaporationModel.calculate_diameter_squared
documentation:
Calculate D² over time using the chosen model.

```

Time array

type ta

array

param Bm

Spalding mass transfer number

type Bm

Float

Choice of droplet evaporation model (d2 law or infinite liquid conductivity), defaults to "d2_law"

str, optional

return

Droplet diameter squared

rtype

array
```
